description: Optional WORLDPRINT candidate intake queue. Add future World Bank indicator batches here before moving mature candidates into the curated built-in list.
instructions: Rows in candidates enter the source gate and generated scorecards immediately.
Rows without curated editorial review default to needs_review and are not playable.
After reviewing generated scorecards, promote strong rows through content/editorial/worldprint-indicator-review.json.

exampleCandidate.dataCaveat: Optional source or unit warning for players.
regionalSignals: Optional pattern hint for reveal copy.

candidates.unit: constant 2015 US dollars per cubic meter
kg CO2e per constant 2015 US dollar

candidates.shortTitle: Women, Business and the Law index

candidates.whyItMatters: Secondary enrollment gender parity can reveal where girls' access to school diverges from boys' access after primary school.
The female share of primary teachers can expose education-workforce patterns that are not the same as enrollment.
Secondary teacher gender mix can differ sharply from primary teaching and from student enrollment patterns.

candidates.shortHook: A classroom-crowding map for older students.
candidates.whyItMatters: Secondary pupil-teacher ratios give players an education-capacity signal that is different from enrollment access.
Lower secondary ratios can reveal education capacity during the transition between primary and upper secondary school.

candidates.shortHook: A school-track map with industrial clues.
candidates.whyItMatters: Vocational enrollment can separate education systems that route more students into technical and career training.
Secondary repeaters reveal education-system friction that can be invisible in broad enrollment maps.

candidates.unit: percent of secondary school age population
candidates.shortHook: A map of who is missing from secondary school.
candidates.whyItMatters: Out-of-school secondary-age shares can be more intuitive than gross enrollment when reading education access.
Education spending as a government-budget share can show policy priority without simply tracking national income.
candidates.dataCaveat: Budget-share maps do not directly measure school quality.

candidates.whyItMatters: Primary education's share of education spending can reveal where school budgets emphasize basic education.
Secondary education's spending share adds budget depth without duplicating enrollment maps.
Upper-secondary attainment shows long-run education outcomes among adults, not just current school enrollment.
The share of tertiary students in science and engineering can add a more surprising education puzzle for advanced players.
Cereal cropland makes wheat, rice, maize, and other grain-growing geography visible without using raw production totals.
Irrigated cropland helps players separate dryland farming from agriculture that depends on managed water.

candidates.unit: tractors per 100 sq. km of arable land
candidates.whyItMatters: Tractors per arable land can reveal mechanized farming systems better than crop area alone.
candidates.dataCaveat: This series may be older or sparse for some countries.

candidates.whyItMatters: Permanent pasture adds land-use variety that is different from arable land, forest cover, or cropland.

candidates.unit: rural people per sq. km of arable land
candidates.whyItMatters: Rural density on arable land connects settlement pressure to the geography of farmable land.

candidates.shortHook: A raw grain-area map for expert review.
candidates.whyItMatters: Land under cereal production can show major grain belts, but raw-area maps need careful editorial handling.
candidates.dataCaveat: Raw land-area totals favor large countries and may be less fair than shares.

candidates.whyItMatters: Urban low-elevation population shows where people live in cities close to sea level.
Rural low-elevation population complements coastal city exposure with a different settlement signal.
Urban low-elevation land can reveal coastal urban geography, deltas, and island exposure.
Rural low-elevation land adds a quieter coastal-exposure map for advanced play.
Rural population growth separates urbanization from the places where rural populations are still rising or shrinking.
ICT service exports can highlight countries where software, telecom, and digital services matter in trade.
Air passengers carried can expose transport hubs and tourism or business travel scale, though raw totals need review.
candidates.dataCaveat: Raw passenger counts favor larger countries and hub economies.

candidates.whyItMatters: Registered air carrier departures can reveal transport connectivity and hub geography.
candidates.dataCaveat: Raw departures favor larger countries and aviation hubs.

candidates.whyItMatters: Container port traffic spotlights maritime trade gateways and logistics-heavy economies.
candidates.dataCaveat: Landlocked countries and non-containerized trade can make this map zero-heavy.

candidates.whyItMatters: Customs efficiency adds a specific logistics signal beyond the broad logistics index.
Tracking and tracing ability can separate logistics systems that otherwise look similar.
Timeliness shows how reliably shipments arrive when expected, not just infrastructure quality.
Part-time employment can reveal labor-market structure in a way unemployment rates do not.
Female part-time employment can expose work-family patterns and labor-market rules that total employment hides.

candidates.shortTitle: Youth not in education or work
candidates.whyItMatters: NEET share captures young people who are neither studying nor working, making it a strong labor-and-education bridge.

candidates.shortTitle: Female youth not in education or work
candidates.whyItMatters: Female NEET rates can reveal where young women are outside both school and paid work.
Youth employment-to-population ratio helps distinguish studying, job access, and labor-market entry patterns.
Own-account work helps separate wage economies from self-directed or informal work patterns.

candidates.unit: percent of senior and middle management
candidates.whyItMatters: Women's share of senior and middle management adds a labor-market equity signal that is not the same as parliament or schooling.

candidates.unit: percent of working-age population with advanced education
candidates.whyItMatters: Labor force participation among adults with advanced education can reveal how education converts into formal labor activity.
The secondary-educated share of the labor force connects school attainment with the structure of work.

candidates.unit: percent of labor force with advanced education
candidates.whyItMatters: Unemployment among advanced-educated workers can produce surprising patterns that differ from total unemployment.

candidates.shortHook: A gender-balance map inside primary classrooms.
candidates.whyItMatters: The female share of primary pupils can reveal education access patterns without using broad literacy or enrollment rates.

candidates.shortHook: A harder gender-balance map in secondary school.
candidates.whyItMatters: The female share of secondary pupils can diverge from primary schooling and from adult education outcomes.
Combined primary and secondary parity gives a broad schooling-balance signal without being identical to a single level.
Gross intake into grade 1 can reveal delayed or repeated school entry patterns that enrollment rates hide.
Repeaters show school-system friction and progression patterns that are different from access alone.

candidates.unit: percent of primary school age population
candidates.shortHook: A map of who is missing from primary school.
candidates.whyItMatters: Out-of-school primary-age shares give players a direct education-access signal.
Compulsory education duration adds a policy-shaped schooling puzzle that is not simply about income.
Primary attainment captures long-run education outcomes among adults rather than current enrollment.
Female employment-to-population ratio connects gender, work, and social structure more directly than total employment.
Male employment-to-population ratio gives a useful contrast to total and female employment patterns.
Women's wage and salaried work share helps distinguish formal labor markets from self-employment and family work.
Men's wage and salaried work share adds a gendered formal-employment comparison for expert play.
Female vulnerable employment shows where own-account and contributing-family work dominate women's jobs.
Male vulnerable employment gives a sharper labor-structure clue than the total vulnerable-employment map alone.
The female share of work in agriculture can diverge strongly from total agricultural employment.
Male agricultural employment helps players compare rural work patterns by gender.
Female industry employment can reveal manufacturing and industrial labor patterns that total employment hides.
Male industry employment gives expert players a clean comparison to total and female industry work.
Female services employment often reveals urban, income, and labor-market structure in a readable pattern.
Male services employment provides a useful counterpart to the total services-employment map.
Contributing family work highlights labor markets where unpaid family work remains common.
Female contributing family work can reveal hidden labor patterns behind broad employment rates.
Male contributing family work is a harder expert map that can separate informal labor systems.
Female self-employment helps distinguish entrepreneurial, informal, and agricultural work structures.
Male self-employment adds a gendered labor-market structure puzzle for advanced play.
Communications and computer service exports can highlight digital-service economies without using raw export totals.
Communications and computer service imports add a different connectivity signal than internet access.
Transport services as a share of exports can expose shipping, aviation, and logistics-specialized economies.
Transport service imports can reveal connectivity costs and trade-service dependence.
Air freight spotlights logistics hubs and export networks that passenger travel does not show.
candidates.dataCaveat: Raw air freight favors aviation hubs and large economies.

candidates.whyItMatters: Urban electricity access can reveal where cities are still not fully connected to basic power.
Urban clean cooking access separates modern city infrastructure from broader energy access.
Urban basic drinking water gives a city-infrastructure puzzle that is easier to read than raw water resources.
Rural basic drinking water highlights infrastructure gaps outside cities.
Urban sanitation access gives a readable infrastructure signal that can differ from drinking water.
Rural sanitation access exposes infrastructure gaps that broad national sanitation can smooth over.
Urban open defecation is a harder settlement-infrastructure clue with a clear unit and strong pattern.
Rural open defecation gives an advanced map of infrastructure gaps outside cities.
Cereal production reveals major grain producers and can work as an expert map when raw-size bias is understood.
candidates.dataCaveat: Raw production totals favor large countries and major exporters.

candidates.whyItMatters: Arable land area shows where large farmable land bases sit, but needs expert framing because it is a raw total.
candidates.dataCaveat: Raw land-area totals favor large countries.

candidates.whyItMatters: Agricultural methane highlights livestock, rice, and other farm systems in a different way than crop maps.
candidates.dataCaveat: Raw emissions favor countries with large agricultural sectors.

candidates.whyItMatters: Agricultural nitrous oxide gives an expert environmental-agriculture signal tied to soils, fertilizer, and livestock.
Moderate or severe food insecurity adds a human food-system map that is not simply crop production.
Severe food insecurity gives advanced players a harder version of the food-security signal.
Food imports as a share of merchandise imports can reveal dependence on imported food and island or arid-country patterns.
Food exports as a share of merchandise exports can highlight agriculture-specialized economies without raw export totals.
